title: Inspect repo docs/skills alignment for note-lead short-teaser policy after runtime exemption. Focus only on these files in /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public: skills/x-auto-post-guard/SKILL.md, docs/agents/x-auto-contract.md, docs/x-auto-note-linking-rule.md. Report exact lines or wording...
summary_text: Here are the remaining assumptions that still hard-tie short note leads to non-schedulable/draft status by 800-char runtime logic: 1. [skills/x-auto-post-guard/SKILL.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/skills/x-auto-post-guard/SKILL.md:92-98) - Wording: - `if a shorter draft is requested for ideation only...` - `if the user wants a truly short note lead that would likely fall below the runtime gate, keep it \`draft\` until the runtime rule changes instead of pretending it is scheduler-safe` - `a sub-800 draft may read fine but still fail later in scheduler validation` - Why this still assumes the gate: it explicitly says sub-800 note leads should stay draft unless a future runtime rule changes (no exception for current runtime exemptions). 2. [docs/agents/x-auto-contract.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/docs/agents/x-auto-contract.md:62-64) - Wording: - `For schedulable draft candidates, prefer the runtime-safe target of \`800-1500字\` unless the user explicitly asks for a non-posting short draft.` - `For rows created on or after \`2026-03-30\`, \`<800字\` is a hard publish blocker.` - `Such rows must not remain \`approved\`; they require rewrite or demotion before the scheduler reaches the slot.` - Why this still assumes the block: `<800字` is described as hard, with no documented exemption branch for note leads. 3. [docs/x-auto-note-linking-rule.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/docs/x-auto-note-linking-rule.md:20-21) - Wording: - `Prefer the shortest curiosity-preserving body that still satisfies the active runtime approval rules.` - `If the desired note lead is intentionally shorter than the current runtime allows for scheduling, keep it as \`draft\` until runtime policy changes.` - Why this still assumes the block: it explicitly forces draft for short note leads whenever current runtime does not allow it. Proposed minimal edits (policy-only, no behavior changes implied): 1) In [SKILL.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/skills/x-auto-post-guard/SKILL.md:92) - Replace the hard fallback sentence with an exemption-aware rule: - `if a truly short note lead falls below the runtime gate, keep it \`draft\` unless a runtime exemption is active for that note lead; if exempted, continue only on explicit approval status review before scheduling` 2) In [x-auto-contract.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/docs/agents/x-auto-contract.md:62-64) - Replace: - `For rows created on or after ... <800字 is a hard publish blocker...` - With: - `For rows created on or after ... <800字 is a hard publish blocker for non-exempt rows.` - `For \`note.com\` lead posts covered by runtime exemption, apply the exempted short-note policy instead of this hard blocker.` - (keep the rewrite/demotion sentence only for the non-exempt branch) 3) In [x-auto-note-linking-rule.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/docs/x-auto-note-linking-rule.md:20-21) - Replace: - `If the desired note lead is intentionally shorter than the current runtime allows for scheduling, keep it as draft until runtime policy changes.` - With: - `If the desired note lead is intentionally shorter than the general scheduling floor, keep it as draft unless the active runtime exemption is in effect, then treat it as normal scheduler eligibility after explicit review checks.` If you want, I can draft the exact one-line diff hunks for these three files next.
